task_completion.visual_evidence: The NextGen Connect Administrator GUI shows a channel named 'Data_Quality_Normalizer' in the Channels or Summary tab.

task_completion.description: Source connector is configured as a TCP Listener on port 6661
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Source tab, the Connector Type is set to 'TCP Listener' and the Local Port is set to '6661'.

task_completion.description: Destination connector is configured as a File Writer to the correct directory and filename
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Destinations tab, the Connector Type is 'File Writer', the Directory is '/tmp/normalized_output/', and the File Name is 'normalized_${message.messageId}.hl7'.

task_completion.description: JavaScript transformers are configured for the normalization rules
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Transformers view (Source or Destination), there is JavaScript code visible that references PID.13 (Phone), PID.7 (DOB), and PID.8 (Gender) implementing the normalization logic.

task_completion.description: The channel is deployed and has processed messages
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Dashboard tab shows the 'Data_Quality_Normalizer' channel with a status of Started/Deployed, and non-zero counts (likely 4) under the Received and Sent columns.

task_completion.description: The normalized output files are generated and contain the correctly transformed data
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window or file viewer shows the contents of the files in '/tmp/normalized_output/', displaying the normalized PID segments (e.g., DOB as YYYYMMDD, Phone as (XXX) XXX-XXXX, Gender as M/F).

integrity.description: Agent used the NextGen Connect Administrator GUI to configure the channel
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the NextGen Connect Administrator Java application interface being used, rather than just XML channel files being edited in a text editor.

integrity.description: The messages were actually processed by the engine
integrity.visual_evidence: The Dashboard shows message statistics (Received/Sent counts) indicating the engine processed the HL7 messages.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the engine to generate outputs
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of a standalone Python or Bash script being executed in the terminal to directly create the output files in '/tmp/normalized_output/'.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
Test data includes 4 HL7 messages. Expected normalized values in the PID segment:
Hospital A: Phone '(555) 867-5309', DOB '19850315', Gender 'M'.
Hospital B: Phone '(555) 234-5678', DOB '19900322', Gender 'F'.
Hospital C: Phone '(555) 987-6543', DOB '19781105', Gender 'M'.
Hospital D: Phone '(555) 345-6789', DOB '20010704', Gender 'M'.
Output files must be located in '/tmp/normalized_output/' with names formatted as 'normalized_<messageId>.hl7'.
